page: Amazon Web Services m8azn.large is a general-purpose instance featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It provides 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs operating at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of memory per core. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, has no local storage, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the m8azn.large demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 operations. Its multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests show average read and write speeds, while the Passmark memory score is weak. The processor supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions, which assist in integer and encryption tasks.

Given its high clock speed and dedicated CPU allocation, the m8azn.large offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for workloads constrained by single-thread performance rather than parallel execution. It is well-suited for applications such as lightweight web serving, text processing, software compilation, and basic database operations. However, it is less optimal for memory-intensive tasks or multi-threaded workloads like ray tracing, where its performance profile is weaker.
description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.large is a general-purpose instance featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 processor with 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 5.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, utilizing a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance, single-threaded compression, and text processing, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. With support for AVX-512 and cryptography extensions, this instance is highly efficient for single-thread-bound workloads, lightweight web serving, software compilation, and basic database operations, though it is less suited for memory-heavy or highly parallelized tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.large is a general-purpose instance with a 5.0 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, 2 vCPUs, and 8 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance for single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.large is a general-purpose instance featuring a 5.0 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, 2 vCPUs, and 8 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM.
tagline: A high-frequency general-purpose virtual server delivering top-tier single-core performance and fast DDR5 memory optimized for demanding single-thread-bound compute workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz
8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for lightweight web serving, text processing, and software compilation
